heroHeadline: Protests Sweep West Bank After Palestinian Authority Enacts Death Penalty Law
heroSubtitle: Widespread demonstrations erupted across West Bank cities on April 7, 2026, as Palestinians protested the Palestinian Authority's new death penalty law. Human rights groups condemned the legislation as authoritarian overreach that could suppress political dissent.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, IDF, Gaza Ministry of Health, B'Tselem, ICC, ICJ, Reuters, Al Jazeera, OHCHR, UNRWA, Yesh Din, Human Rights Watch, and institutional analyses. Casualty figures are subject to ongoing verification. The conflict has persisted since Israeli independence on May 14, 1948.
